followup_notes.changes_from_prior_cycle: Cycle 1 review; no prior cycle to compare.

main_claims.claim: The three HDE densities generate explicit wormhole shape functions satisfying the throat condition and asymptotic flatness.
main_claims.notes: The integrations for the Rényi and Moradpour profiles look plausible up to an additive constant, but the paper sets the integration constant equal to r_th rather than choosing it so that epsilon(r_th)=r_th. The asymptotic claim also depends on the correct normalization and parameter choices.
main_claims.paper_location: Sec. IV, Eqs. (11), (14)-(16), and text after Eq. (16), arXiv PDF https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.18246

main_claims.claim: The constructed geometries satisfy flaring-out and violate the radial NEC in the required region.
main_claims.notes: At the throat the condition reduces to epsilon'(r_th)<1, equivalently rho+p_r=(epsilon'(r_th)-1)/(8 pi r_th^2)<0 for finite redshift. The paper does not derive parameter inequalities ensuring this for the three positive-density profiles.
main_claims.paper_location: Sec. IV, Eqs. (5), (11), (12), and surrounding discussion

main_claims.claim: The hyperbolic redshift functions are finite at the throat and asymptotically flat.
main_claims.notes: They are finite, but Phi_Cosh tends Phi_0 and Phi_Tanh tends Phi_0 as r goes to infinity under the stated formulas, contradicting the earlier normalization Phi->0. Phi_Sinh decays only under an unstated condition on r_c and r_0.
main_claims.paper_location: Sec. V, Eqs. (19)-(21) and the sentence immediately after Eq. (21)

main_claims.claim: Slow rotation produces the stated frame-dragging, photon-orbit splitting, and photon-sphere shifts.
main_claims.notes: The geodesic formulas contain inconsistent powers of e^Phi, the photon-sphere condition is not consistently presented with both R=0 and R'=0, and the numerical values used are not clearly within the slow-rotation regime.

main_claims.claim: Rényi-supported wormholes produce smaller, more asymmetric shadows, while mixed and Moradpour profiles produce larger, nearly circular silhouettes.
main_claims.notes: No full shadow construction in celestial coordinates, observer inclination, ray-tracing setup, or parameter table is provided. The redshift profiles are introduced independently of the three HDE density profiles, so the attribution of shadow morphology to Rényi versus mixed/Moradpour matter is not demonstrated.
main_claims.paper_location: Abstract, Sec. V.C, and Sec. VI

missing_assumptions.assumption: Explicit parameter ranges for alpha, lambda, r_th, Phi_0, r_0, r_c, and J that simultaneously ensure epsilon(r_th)=r_th, epsilon'(r_th)<1, epsilon(r)/r<1 outside the throat, Phi finite and normalized at infinity, and |omega r|<<1.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The existence of a traversable wormhole and the validity of the perturbative photon-dynamics formulas depend on these inequalities.

missing_assumptions.assumption: A stress-energy model beyond the density rho(r), including radial/tangential pressures or an equation of state, and the rotational T_{t phi} sector supporting omega(r).
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Specifying only rho(r) and an arbitrary redshift/rotation function does not establish a self-consistent Einstein solution sourced solely by the proposed HDE matter.

missing_assumptions.assumption: A defined relation, if any, between the three HDE density profiles and the three hyperbolic redshift profiles.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Without this relation, conclusions about Rényi versus mixed/Moradpour observational signatures are not derived from the HDE models.

missing_assumptions.assumption: A precise shadow-observer setup: asymptotic normalization, observer screen coordinates, inclination, photon constants of motion, and numerical parameter values used for the figures.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Shadow morphology is observer- and setup-dependent; impact-parameter splitting alone is insufficient to justify the stated silhouettes.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Check the original LaTeX for Eq. (15), the mixed HDE shape function, because PDF extraction makes part of the expression ambiguous.
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The local objection about the throat constant is independent of this ambiguity, but a human should verify whether the mixed-profile integral itself is printed correctly.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Verify the derivation of the photon-sphere shift formula Eq. (38) in proper radial coordinates near a wormhole throat.
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The paper gives no derivation, and a careful GR calculation is needed to decide whether the stated coefficient and sign are correct.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Check whether the cited Teo/Hartle slow-rotation framework permits the chosen omega(r)=2J/r^3 with the unspecified HDE stress tensor at linear order.
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: This depends on the full stationary-axisymmetric Einstein equations and matter model, which the paper does not provide.

novelty_assessment: The combination of three HDE-inspired density profiles with a slowly rotating Teo-type wormhole ansatz may be a modestly new phenomenological package, but the novelty is hard to assess because the paper mostly applies standard wormhole and null-geodesic formulas and does not establish a self-consistent mapping from HDE densities to the chosen redshift/rotation functions. The strong 'first time' and observational-signature claims are not well supported by the derivations shown.
score_rationale: The topic is potentially interesting, but several central claims are not locally supported: the throat condition is mishandled, NEC/flaring bounds are missing, the asymptotic behavior of redshift functions conflicts with the stated assumptions, the slow-rotation examples are outside the claimed perturbative regime, and the shadow conclusions are not backed by a defined shadow calculation. These are not cosmetic issues; they affect the existence of the modeled wormholes and the photon-dynamics conclusions.
summary: The paper studies Teo-type slowly rotating wormholes sourced by three holographic-dark-energy density profiles and then discusses null geodesics, photon spheres, frame dragging, and putative shadows. I do not certify the whole proof. My local check found several auditable gaps: the throat normalization of the shape functions appears wrong, the flaring/NEC conditions are not actually established for the parameter ranges, the redshift profiles are not consistently asymptotically flat as stated, and the photon-dynamics/shadow formulas contain unsupported or inconsistent steps.

technical_objections.evidence: The paper writes epsilon_model(r)=F_model(r)+epsilon_th and then states epsilon_th=r_th. But the throat condition requires F_model(r_th)+epsilon_th=r_th, so generally epsilon_th should be r_th-F_model(r_th). For example, the Moradpour F_model(r_th) is proportional to arctan(sqrt(pi lambda) r_th), nonzero for positive r_th.
technical_objections.objection: The additive constants in the shape functions do not enforce the throat condition as stated.
technical_objections.paper_location: Sec. IV, Eqs. (14)-(16) and text after Eq. (16)

technical_objections.evidence: For finite Phi, the throat condition gives rho+p_r=(epsilon'(r_th)-1)/(8 pi r_th^2). The three HDE densities have positive epsilon'(r_th), and NEC violation/flaring requires model-specific bounds such as 2 alpha/(1+pi lambda r_th^2)<1 for the Moradpour profile. Analogous inequalities for the Rényi and mixed profiles are not stated or checked.
technical_objections.objection: Flaring-out and NEC violation are asserted without the needed parameter restrictions.

technical_objections.evidence: Eq. (6) requires Phi(r)->0. However Phi_Cosh=Phi_0[1-sech(r/r_0)] and Phi_Tanh=Phi_0 tanh(r/r_0) tend Phi_0, not 0. Phi_Sinh behaves like exp(r(1/r_0-1/r_c)) and decays only if r_c<r_0, which is not assumed.
technical_objections.objection: The redshift functions are not consistently asymptotically flat under the paper's own normalization.
technical_objections.paper_location: Sec. II Eq. (6) and Sec. V Eqs. (19)-(21)

technical_objections.evidence: Solving E=e^{2Phi} tdot+r^2 omega phidot and L=r^2(phidot-omega tdot) to first order gives tdot=(E-omega L)e^{-2Phi} and rdot^2=(1-epsilon/r)[E^2 e^{-2Phi}(1-omega b)^2-L^2/r^2]+O(omega^2). Eq. (27) instead contains a factor (1-omega b e^Phi)^2 inside an overall e^{-2Phi}, while Eqs. (28)-(29) use different powers/signs of e^Phi. This changes the first-order frame-dragging correction.
technical_objections.objection: The null geodesic radial equation appears not to follow from the conserved quantities and is internally inconsistent with later formulas.

technical_objections.evidence: Eq. (30) is essentially the radial turning-point equation. A circular photon orbit also requires extremality of the radial potential, dR/dr=0, and stability classification requires a second-derivative check. The derivative condition appears only later and no derivation is given for the shift formulas, especially the dependence on epsilon'(r_th) in Eq. (38).
technical_objections.objection: The photon-sphere calculation uses an incomplete or unsupported condition.
technical_objections.paper_location: Sec. V.C, Eqs. (30)-(38), and Sec. VI lines restating the condition

technical_objections.evidence: With omega≈2J/r^3, the expansion parameter near the quoted throats is |omega r|=2J/r^2. The listed examples give about 1.25 for Rényi (r_th=0.2,J=0.025), 0.82 for mixed (0.35,0.05), and 0.60 for Moradpour (0.5,0.075), none of which is clearly much smaller than one. The figures go up to J=0.7, making the linearized treatment even less controlled near small radii.
technical_objections.objection: The numerical examples violate the stated slow-rotation assumption.
technical_objections.paper_location: Sec. V.A representative values, Figs. 1-2 captions, and Sec. VI statement |omega r_ph| << 1

technical_objections.evidence: The sinh/cosh/tanh redshift profiles are introduced as illustrative functions, not derived from the Rényi, mixed, or Moradpour densities or from an equation of state. Nevertheless, the conclusions attribute photon-sphere and shadow behavior to cuspy Rényi versus smoother mixed/Moradpour profiles. That mapping is not established by the field equations presented.
technical_objections.objection: The paper conflates independent redshift choices with the HDE density models when drawing physical conclusions.
technical_objections.paper_location: Sec. V Eqs. (19)-(21), Sec. V.C discussion, and Sec. VI conclusions

technical_objections.evidence: The paper gives impact-parameter formulas and plots labeled photon orbits/photon-sphere profiles, but does not define celestial coordinates, observer location/inclination, screen mapping, emitting region, or ray-tracing procedure. The claims about smaller/asymmetric versus larger/circular shadows therefore exceed the shown calculation.
technical_objections.objection: The claimed shadow calculation is not actually specified.
technical_objections.paper_location: Abstract, Sec. V.C, Figs. 1-2, and Sec. VI

technical_objections.evidence: Sec. V.C states that increasing spin pushes prograde orbits outward and retrograde orbits inward, while Sec. VI states that slow rotation shifts co-rotating photons inward and counter-rotating photons outward. These cannot both be correct without a convention change that is not explained.
technical_objections.objection: There is a direct sign/direction inconsistency in the qualitative interpretation of frame-dragging shifts.
technical_objections.paper_location: Sec. V.C text around Figs. 1-2 and Sec. VI
